core_model.structure_summary: A coupled slow-fast system of stochastic differential equations whose drift and diffusion coefficients depend on the law of the process itself, driven by Brownian motion and a compensated Poisson jump measure, with two separated small time-scale parameters.

variables.meaning: slow component and fast component of the coupled process
Brownian increments and a compensated Poisson jump measure
small noise-intensity and small time-scale-separation parameters, plus the distribution (law) of the state entering the coefficients

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A first-order ordinary differential equation for the slow variable with an averaged, distribution-collapsed (Dirac-measure) drift, obtained as the small-noise, time-scale-averaged limit.

notes: Core is a two-time-scale McKean-Vlasov (distribution-dependent) SDE system with Poisson jumps; the paper's large deviation / averaging result shows the small-parameter limit concentrates on the Dirac measure of the solution to a deterministic ODE, which is the skeleton. Coefficients depend on state so noise is multiplicative; the fast equation's negative drift (contraction constant C_5) supports damping_term.
